title: Cheap Thrills: Why These 3 Entertainment Stocks Are Soaring
author: Dan Schmidt
description: Despite consumer pessimism about the economy, Americans are redirecting spending toward affordable domestic entertainment. Three entertainment stocks—Marcus Corporation (MCS), Six Flags Entertainment (FUN), and Sphere Entertainment (SPHR)—are outperforming the broader market with gains exceeding 50% year-to-date. Each company has successfully raised prices without suppressing volume, driven by premium experiences, activist turnarounds, and profitable operations respectively.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong comparable admissions growth (23.6% YOY in Q1 2026), successful premium pricing strategy (12.7% ticket price increase), declining operating expenses, solid cash position ($194M), and positive technical indicators (Golden Cross, bullish MACD crossover) support continued upward momentum.

Activist investment from Jana Partners driving turnaround, narrower-than-expected losses, 12% YOY revenue growth, positive attendance (4%) and per capita spending (6%) growth, asset sales improving operational flexibility, and strong technical recovery (60% YTD gains, Golden Cross, RSI confirmation).

Surprising profitability in Q4 2025 (EPS $1.23 vs. expected loss), 37% YOY revenue growth in Q1 2026, top-grossing Vegas venue status, successful shows (Wizard of Oz with 3M tickets), East Coast expansion plans, and healthy technical uptrend with consistent support and strong RSI performance.

title: Wix Stock Down 70% in One Year, and This $7 Million Investor Called It Quits
author: Jonathan Ponciano
description: Metavasi Capital sold its entire 37,000-share stake in Wix.com in Q4 2025, reducing its position value by $6.57 million. The sale comes as Wix stock has declined 70% over the past year, significantly underperforming the S&P 500's 15% gain. Despite solid fundamentals including 14% revenue growth and $127 million in free cash flow, the investor opted to reallocate capital to other high-beta growth opportunities.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock has declined 70% over the past year, significantly underperforming the broader market. A major investor exited its entire position, signaling reduced confidence despite the company's solid operational metrics and revenue growth.
Mentioned as a top holding in Metavasi Capital's portfolio after the Wix exit, representing 9.1% of AUM. No specific performance data provided in the article.
Listed as a top holding representing 8.6% of AUM. Characterized as a high-beta growth name but no specific performance metrics provided.
Mentioned as a top holding representing 8.3% of AUM. Described as a high-beta growth name without specific performance data.

title: Optimum-MSGN Standoff Could Pressure MSG Networks' Finances: Analyst
author: Lekha Gupta
description: The carriage dispute between Optimum and MSG Networks could negatively impact MSG Networks' financials, but potentially benefit Sphere Entertainment by reducing its debt. The analyst sees the loss of Optimum carriage as a net positive for Sphere Entertainment.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The analyst sees the loss of Optimum carriage as a net positive for Sphere Entertainment, potentially pushing MSG Networks toward a full default scenario and clearing over $700 million in non-recourse debt from the company's balance sheet, boosting its valuation.
The analyst writes that the bankruptcy for the RSN could reduce local rights fees and related cash flow for Madison Square Garden Sports, which owns the Knicks and Rangers.
Altice USA's Optimum brand is in a carriage dispute with MSG Networks, which could impact the financials of both companies.

title: Best Stock to Buy Right Now: Sphere Entertainment vs. Walt Disney
author: Anders Bylund
description: Disney and Sphere Entertainment are both entertainment giants undergoing strategic changes. Disney's stock looks more affordable and stable, while Sphere Entertainment's ambitious growth plans come with heavy financial risks.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Disney is a longtime stalwart in the entertainment industry, with a strong content portfolio and a focus on streaming media. Its stock is considered affordable and a safe investment option.

Sphere Entertainment's ambitious plans for global entertainment venues come with heavy financial risks, including high capital expenses and debt repayment challenges. The company is currently unprofitable, and its stock is seen as a risky investment.

title: Sphere Entertainment Stock Soared This Week. Here's Why.
author: Brett Schafer
description: Sphere Entertainment, a Las Vegas-based entertainment venue, saw its revenue grow from near zero to $150 million in just one quarter. However, the company is still burning a significant amount of cash, making it a risky investment despite its upside potential.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article highlights Sphere Entertainment's strong revenue growth, with revenue from its Las Vegas Sphere venue growing from near zero to $151 million in just one quarter. This indicates the venue's popularity and demand for its unique offerings.

While the article notes Sphere Entertainment's strong revenue growth, it also highlights the company's significant cash burn, with an operating loss of $71.4 million in the quarter. This makes the company's investment proposition risky, despite its upside potential.
